meta.title: Control4 TV Driver Compatibility Reference
meta.description: Comprehensive C4 integration method lookup by TV brand and model line. Used by Bob AI assistant to validate client TV selections against Control4 compatibility.
meta.compiler_notes: Data sourced from Control4 Driver Search database (drivers.control4.com), Chowmain Software, DriverCentral, CE Pro, Residential Systems, and Reddit/Control4 community. Samsung QNF-series (2025) certified IP drivers confirmed October 2025. Hisense VIDAA Chowmain driver launched July 25, 2025. Vizio DriverCentral driver released February 10, 2026. LG C5/G5/B5 2025 native IP drivers confirmed July 2025.

integration_levels.1: Native SDDP (IP) — TV auto-discovers on network, two-way IP control, no extra hardware. Best.
integration_levels.2: 3rd-Party IP Driver — Requires Chowmain or DriverCentral purchased driver. Works but dependent on third-party updates.
integration_levels.3: IR Only — Requires physical IR flasher + wire run per location. One-way, no feedback.
integration_levels.4: No Driver — Cannot be controlled from C4 at all.

brands.brand_notes: Samsung has been a Control4 SDDP partner since 2017. All current mainstream 4K lines (QLED, Neo QLED, QD-OLED, Crystal UHD, The Frame) have native IP drivers created by Control4, certified, with SDDP auto-discovery. Samsung TVs purchased from warehouse clubs (Costco, Sam's Club) may use stripped model numbers that don't match standard driver entries and may not SDDP-discover — use retail model numbers. Newer 2025 QNF-series drivers confirmed October 24, 2025.

models.dealer_channel: Retail (Best Buy, Samsung.com, etc.) — No Snap One/Mountain West exclusive. Driver is free in C4 ecosystem.
models.notes: Confirmed drivers QN65QN90FAFXZA, QN75QN90FAFXZA, QN75QN90FDFXZA. Top-tier Neo QLED with Mini LED, 4.2.2 sound. Auto-discovers via SDDP. Best C4 integration experience.
Confirmed drivers QN65QN80FAFXZA, QN75QN80FAFXZA, QN75QN80FDFXZA. Mid-tier Neo QLED. SDDP auto-discovers.
Confirmed drivers QN75QN70FAFXZA, QN75QN70FDFXZA, QN85QN70FAFXZA. Entry-tier Neo QLED. SDDP auto-discovers.
Confirmed QN65Q80DAFXZA, QN75Q80DAFXZA, QN85Q80DAFXZA. Standard QLED, VA panel. SDDP auto-discovers.
Confirmed QN65Q70DAFXZA, QN65Q72DDFXZA. SDDP auto-discovers.
Confirmed QN50Q60DAFXZA, QN65Q60DAFXZA, QN70Q60DAFXZA, QN32Q60DAFXZA. Entry QLED. SDDP auto-discovers.
Confirmed QN55S95DAFXZA, QN65S95DAFXZA, QN77S95DAFXZA. Flagship QD-OLED. VDE 'Real Black' certified display. Glare-Free 2.0. SDDP auto-discovers.
models.bob_recommendation: APPROVE — Premium QD-OLED with full C4 integration.

models.notes: Confirmed QN55S90DAFXZA, QN65S90DAFXZA, QN65S90DDFXZA. SDDP auto-discovers.
Confirmed QN55S85DDEXZA, QN65S85DAEXZA, QN65S85DDEXZA, QN77S85DDEXZA, QN83S85DAEXZA, QN83S85DDEXZA. SDDP auto-discovers.
Confirmed QN43LS03TAFXZA, QN75LS03DAFXZA, QN75LS03DDFXZA, QN85LS03FWFXZA. Art mode TV. Full IP/SDDP. The Frame 2025 (LS03F) also has confirmed drivers as of Oct 2025.

Confirmed UN43DU6900FXZA, UN55DU7200DXZA, UN55DU7200FXZA, UN55DU8000BXZA, UN55DU8000DXZA, UN55DU8000FXZA. Entry-level UHD. SDDP auto-discovers. Warehouse club variants (DU6900 sold at Costco etc.) may not SDDP-discover — use standard retail model number for driver match.
models.bob_recommendation: APPROVE — verify model number is retail (not warehouse variant).

brands.brand_notes: Sony BRAVIA XR and X-series TVs (2019+) support Control4 SDDP and native IP control. Requires manual activation on the TV: Settings > Network > IP Control, enable Simple IP Control and set Pre-Shared Key (PSK), then toggle Control4 SDDP on. Firmware updates have occasionally broken SDDP discovery but the fix is to manually set TV IP in Composer and enter the PSK. All current XR-series (BRAVIA 9, 8, 7) have certified IP drivers. 2025 XR-series (XR50 suffix) drivers confirmed July 2025.

models.notes: Confirmed K-65XR90, K-75XR90, K-85XR90, Y-65XR90, Y-75XR90, Y-85XR90. Sony's flagship Mini LED 4K. Requires PSK setup on TV. SDDP can require manual IP entry if firmware disabled discovery.
models.bob_recommendation: APPROVE — flagship performance, full C4 integration.

models.notes: Confirmed K-65XR80, K-77XR80, K-77XR80C, K-65XR80CM2, Y-55XR80, Y-65XR80, Y-77XR80, Y-85XR80. Sony OLED. Requires PSK setup. Full two-way IP.
Confirmed K-65XR70, K-75XR70, K-85XR70, Y-55XR70, Y-65XR70, Y-75XR70, Y-85XR70. Requires PSK setup.
Confirmed K-55XR50, K-65XR50, K-75XR50, K-85XR50, K-98XR50, Y-55XR50, Y-65XR50, Y-75XR50, Y-85XR50, K-85XR51Z, K-75XR51Z. New 2025 entry BRAVIA tier added July 2025.
Confirmed XR-65X90L, XR-75X90L, XR-65X91L, XR-75X91L. Requires PSK setup.
Confirmed XR-65A83L, XR-65A84L, XR-65X93CL, XR-65X93L. Full IP/SDDP with PSK.

brands.brand_notes: LG has supported Control4 SDDP since 2018 (OLED and Super UHD lines). All current webOS LG TVs (OLED, QNED, NanoCell, UQ-series 4K and above) have native certified IP drivers in the C4 database. IP control must be enabled in LG TV settings (Settings > General > IP Control). LG is sold and trained through Snap One partner stores and has a dedicated CI channel portal (reachlg.com). A third-party Chowmain driver also exists for LG WebOS and is preferred by some integrators for faster power-on response.

models.dealer_channel: Available via Snap One dealer channel (LG CI portal / reachlg.com). Retail also available.
models.notes: Confirmed OLED55G5PCA, OLED77G5PSA (modified July 15, 2025). Gallery series evo OLED. Native C4 IP driver. Free. Some integrators use Chowmain LG driver for faster power-on after standby.
models.bob_recommendation: APPROVE — premium OLED, full native C4 integration.

models.dealer_channel: Available via Snap One dealer channel. Retail also available.
models.notes: Confirmed OLED55C5PSA (webOS 25), OLED77C5PSA (modified July 15, 2025). Mainstream OLED. Native C4 IP driver, free. Most popular LG OLED for CI.
models.bob_recommendation: APPROVE — best value OLED for C4 projects.

models.notes: 2025 entry OLED. Native C4 IP driver. Full two-way control.
Confirmed OLED48C4xLA, OLED55C4xLA, OLED65C4xLA, OLED55B4ELA, OLED65B4xLA, OLED55G4xLS (G4). Extensive model variant coverage. webOS 24. Native C4 IP driver.
Confirmed 65QNED996QB, 75QNED99VPA, 86QNED99VPA, 65QNED916QA. QNED93 (Evo Mini LED) and QNED86 launched May 2025. All 4K QNED models have IP control per LG CI training. Native C4 drivers free.
models.bob_recommendation: APPROVE — good high-brightness option for bright rooms.

models.notes: Confirmed 50NANO763QA, 50NANO766QA, 55NANO763QA, 55NANO766QA, 65NANO763QA, 65NANO766QA. Entry LG 4K. Per LG CI training, all 4K models have IP control. LG replaced UHD base tier with NanoCell in 2025.
models.bob_recommendation: APPROVE — entry tier but full C4 integration.

models.notes: Confirmed 43UQ76906LE, 43UQ76909LE. Legacy 2022 tier. Still in service. Native C4 IP driver. Discontinued new production.

brands.brand_notes: TCL does not have a native SDDP partnership with Control4. The primary C4 integration path is a universal driver published on DriverCentral by Cindev, supporting both IP and IR. A separate DriverCentral entry exists specifically for TCL Google TV models with local IP control. TCL Roku TV models can also use generic Roku TV drivers. TCL is a retail-only brand — not sold through Snap One or Mountain West dealer channel.

models.dealer_channel: Retail only (Amazon, Best Buy, Costco). Not available through Snap One.
models.notes: TCL's flagship QLED Mini LED. Uses DriverCentral universal TCL driver. Known limitation: IP driver does not auto-reconnect after power failure until TV is manually turned on (TCL aware, fix pending). Requires DriverCentral Cloud Driver in project.
models.bob_recommendation: CONDITIONAL — budget-friendly but 3rd-party driver dependency and reconnect caveat. Not recommended for primary display in demanding installs.

models.driver_product: TCL TV — Universal Driver or TCL Google TV Driver
models.notes: Google TV OS models. DriverCentral TCL Google TV driver provides local IP control. Same reconnect limitation after power failure. QM751G uses MediaTek Pentonic 700 SoC.

models.integration_label: IR Only (preferred) or basic Roku IP
models.control_method: IR or basic IP via generic Roku driver
models.notes: TCL Roku TV models (S4, 4-Series) can use generic Roku TV IP driver in C4, but feedback and control are limited. IR is more reliable but requires IR flasher hardware. No dedicated model-specific driver. Avoid for C4 projects requiring reliable two-way feedback.
models.bob_recommendation: AVOID for new projects — use IP-capable model line or different brand.

brands.brand_notes: Hisense has two C4 integration paths depending on model/OS. (1) VIDAA OS models (U-series, some A-series): Chowmain VIDAA driver released July 25, 2025 — IP-based, two-way, includes app launching. Also, VIDAA-created drivers exist in the Control4 certified driver database (modified Feb 6, 2025) and Hisense-created native drivers (modified July 24, 2025) for specific U-series models. (2) Non-VIDAA/Android TV models: IR only. Hisense is a retail brand only, not sold through Snap One or Mountain West dealer channel.

models.driver_source: Chowmain (VIDAA driver) + native certified driver in C4 DB
models.driver_product: Hisense VIDAA Smart TV Driver for Control4
models.dealer_channel: Retail only (Best Buy, Amazon, Costco). Not available through Snap One.
models.notes: Hisense:TV:98U9LUA and 85U9LUA confirmed in C4 certified DB (Hisense creator, modified July 24, 2025). Chowmain VIDAA driver released July 25, 2025 — provides power, volume, input, transport, channel, app launch (Netflix, YouTube, Prime, Disney+), real-time status feedback. 90-day trial. Chowmain driver relatively new — monitor for updates. VIDAA drivers also exist natively (VIDAA_TV_HL7NULTRA, VIDAA_TV_HL7NPRO created Feb 6, 2025).
models.bob_recommendation: CONDITIONAL — newer driver ecosystem. Good integration but 3rd-party dependency.

models.driver_source: Chowmain (VIDAA driver) + native certified driver in C4 DB
models.driver_product: Hisense VIDAA Smart TV Driver for Control4
models.notes: Hisense:TV:85U86LUA confirmed in C4 certified DB. Full VIDAA IP control via Chowmain driver. Two-way feedback. 90-day trial on Chowmain driver.
models.bob_recommendation: CONDITIONAL — solid budget option with workable C4 integration.

models.driver_product: Hisense VIDAA Smart TV Driver for Control4
models.notes: Hisense:TV:85U75LUA in C4 certified DB. VIDAA OS. Full IP control via Chowmain driver. Driver very new (July 2025).

models.driver_product: Hisense VIDAA Smart TV Driver for Control4
models.notes: Entry ULED, VIDAA OS. Compatible with Chowmain VIDAA driver. Driver released July 2025, monitor for stability.

models.driver_source: Control4 IR (generic) or Chowmain Android TV driver
models.control_method: IR or limited IP via Android TV driver
models.notes: Hisense A-series uses Android TV or Google TV OS, not VIDAA. Not compatible with Chowmain VIDAA driver. Chowmain Android TV driver may provide some IP control but is generic. IR flasher recommended for reliable control. No native C4 certified driver.
models.bob_recommendation: AVOID for new projects — use VIDAA U-series if Hisense required.

brands.brand_notes: Vizio does not have a native Control4 SDDP integration. The only C4 driver is a third-party IP driver released February 10, 2026 on DriverCentral by Solidified Systems (MSRP $125). Works with Vizio OS (formerly SmartCast) models. Vizio is sold through retail channels only — not available through Snap One or Mountain West dealer channel. Vizio's product line status: Vizio was acquired by Walmart in 2023 and continues to sell through Walmart, Best Buy, and other retailers. No new high-end lines post-acquisition.

models.dealer_channel: Retail only (Walmart, Best Buy). Not available through Snap One.
models.notes: Vizio OS (SmartCast). DriverCentral driver (released Feb 10, 2026) provides discrete power, volume, input switching, channel control, navigation, mini app launch. VERY new driver — released Feb 2026. Requires DriverCentral Cloud Driver. No SDDP. MSRP $125.
models.bob_recommendation: CONDITIONAL — extremely new driver, insufficient field track record. Not recommended for demanding projects. Consider Samsung/LG instead.

models.notes: Vizio OS (SmartCast). Same driver as M-Series. Very new (Feb 2026). Entry tier. Limited C4 install base.
models.bob_recommendation: AVOID — driver too new, budget tier, no C4 track record.

models.driver_source: Control4 IR (legacy) or DriverCentral IP driver
models.notes: Older P-series. DriverCentral Vizio driver should cover SmartCast models. Pre-SmartCast models: IR only. Vizio P-series discontinued in current lineup.
models.bob_recommendation: NOT RECOMMENDED — discontinued line, driver very new.
